title: How streaming creators built a new broadcast blueprint at the World Cup
tldr: Livestreaming creators offered new ways to broadcast the 2026 World Cup to diverse audiences, potentially becoming the template leagues use going forward. A concrete look at how media consumption is shifting from broadcast to creator-led.
why_fresh: new format trend in live sports broadcasting
angle: Write about how creator-led livestreaming is dismantling the traditional broadcast model and what that means for brand storytelling.

title: Forecasting the future: how can publishers thrive in the AI search era?
tldr: With 42% of UK searches now generating AI summaries and zero-click environments spreading, the traffic model that funded a decade of digital publishing is being dismantled. Most publishers are still optimizing for a model that's disappearing.
angle: Explore what content strategists must change when discovery moves from clicks to AI citations.

title: 80% of What ChatGPT Cites Doesn't Rank on Google. The Great Flattening Just Got a Price Tag.
tldr: A striking finding that most ChatGPT citations don't align with Google rankings, forcing a rethink of visibility strategy. Signals a real divergence between traditional SEO and AI-answer optimization.
why_fresh: new data on AI citation vs search divergence
angle: Unpack why the old SEO playbook fails for AI answer engines and how content visibility is being repriced.

title: Vereigen Media Highlights the Importance of Human-Verified Engagement in an AI-Saturated B2B Marketing Landscape
tldr: Argues that human-verified engagement is becoming the differentiator as AI floods B2B marketing with synthetic activity. Touches directly on the humanity-over-polish theme.
why_fresh: promotional but rides a real human-content trend
angle: Make the case for why human-verified, people-first content beats AI volume in an oversaturated feed.

title: Why Farmers Insurance flipped its jingle as part of all-pink rebrand
tldr: Farmers Insurance overhauled its jingle and went all-pink to escape the 'sea of sameness' in insurance. A concrete campaign breakdown on distinctiveness in a commoditized category.
angle: Analyze how brands break category sameness through sonic and color distinctiveness.

title: Anthropic invites the hard questions about AI in latest Claude campaign
tldr: Anthropic's Claude campaign leans into the uncomfortable questions about AI rather than dodging them. A notable example of brand-purpose positioning through candor.
angle: Explore how leaning into hard questions builds trust better than reassurance in AI comms.

title: WTF is SPUR's publisher-run Content Telemetry Framework?
tldr: SPUR is a publisher-run initiative to turn AI's opaque content scraping into transparent, usage-based licensing that publishers control. A concrete attempt to fix content monetization in the AI era.
angle: Discuss whether publisher-controlled content licensing can rebuild media business models against AI scraping.

title: Dallas' digital creator economy is booming. Burnout is too
tldr: Dallas's creator economy is thriving even as burnout among creators surges. A human-centered look at the cost behind the creator boom.
angle: Write about the sustainability crisis behind the creator economy and what it means for content ops.

title: Koto launches CCType foundry to create typefaces that meet the demands of modern brands
tldr: Creative studio Koto expanded into type design with a foundry building bespoke type systems for modern brands. A signal that custom typography is becoming core brand infrastructure.
angle: Explore how custom type systems are becoming a strategic brand asset, not just decoration.

title: Companies Are Paying Human Salaries and Budgeting AI Hours
tldr: Organizations are starting to budget for AI 'hours' alongside human salaries, a shift in how work and labor are conceptualized. Signals a structural change in content and comms operations.
why_fresh: new operating-model framing for AI labor
angle: Examine what budgeting 'AI hours' means for content team structure and human roles.

title: Can stories save us? JAIKU is putting 60 people in a room to find out
tldr: London agency JAIKU's Storytelling Summit gathers founders, marketers and creative leaders to ask whether narrative still has power to change things. A pulse check on the state of business storytelling.
angle: Question whether storytelling is still a differentiator or has become a hollow buzzword.

title: Sky's ITV deal could shift TV from channel to audience buying
tldr: Sky's planned ITV takeover could accelerate the move from buying TV by channel to buying by audience. A structural shift in how media is planned and bought.
why_fresh: potential structural shift in TV buying
angle: Explore what audience-first TV buying means for how brands plan omnichannel content.

title: An open love letter to advertising: Part 1
tldr: A reflective, human take on why advertising still matters amid AI and automation. A craft-and-humanity piece against the industrialization of creative.
angle: Argue for keeping human craft and emotion at the center of advertising in the AI age.

title: Trust in media by country 2026
tldr: Statista's 2026 data on media trust across countries, useful benchmark for understanding how audiences relate to news and content. Grounding data for any media-consumption argument.
angle: Use trust-by-country data to argue how declining media trust reshapes brand comms strategy.
